title: How Trump Failed to Secure the Strait of Hormuz in His Iran Deal
summary: President Trump signed an agreement that Iran said gave it control of the waterway — and global energy supplies. Now, Iran’s military is violently asserting authority.

title: Lindsey Graham, Republican Senator and Trump Ally, Dies From ‘Sudden Illness’
summary: He died from “a brief and sudden illness,” his office said. Over more than two decades in the Senate, he consistently pushed for the use of U.S. military power overseas.

title: Lindsey Graham Was Facing Re-election in November. What Happens Now?
summary: South Carolina law suggests his death triggers an Aug. 11 special Republican primary election, and the state’s governor can appoint a replacement to serve out the rest of his term, through early January.

title: Trump Recalls Last Conversation With Lindsey Graham Before His Death
summary: In an interview on Sunday, President Trump recounted his last conversation with Senator Lindsey Graham, hours before his death. Mr. Graham sounded “perfect,” he said.

title: How Putin Turned Japan Into a Den of Spies
summary: Operating out of a Tokyo high-rise, a military intelligence unit finds the high-tech equipment that Russia needs to wage war.

title: Zelensky Announces Leadership Shuffle as War Turns in Ukraine’s Favor
summary: President Volodymyr Zelensky said Ukraine’s prime minister, Yuliia Svyrydenko, would step down amid a broader shake-up in Ukraine’s senior leadership.

title: Blanche Stares Down Confirmation Hurdle: Lingering G.O.P. Doubts
summary: Key Republicans on the Judiciary Committee could push for concessions from Todd Blanche, in line to be attorney general, though they did not appear in revolt.

title: Trump Sought an Iran War Exit. Putin Pushed On in Ukraine. Now Both Are Stuck.
summary: The Iran and Ukraine wars underline the common limits of military force in achieving political ends, but also the differences between a dug-in Russian president and a vacillating American one.

title: Trump Justifies $2 Billion Made as President With Inaccurate Claims
summary: President Trump has wrongly attributed the $2 billion windfall he gained during his second term to a hot stock market and claimed that he was the only president to donate his salary.

title: What We Know About the ICE Shooting of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo
summary: Mr. Araujo was a father, a husband and a business owner who had moved to the United States 35 years ago from Mexico.

title: The Democratic Socialists Winning Elections Far From New York City
summary: They won Democratic state legislative primaries in Buffalo and Syracuse, showing how the party’s messaging and ground game can work outside New York City.

title: Younger Voters Are Propelling the Democratic Socialist Surge in New York
summary: Age seemed to be the predominant factor in the Democratic Socialists of America’s primary wins in House races in New York City.

title: How an Election Fight in Arizona Could Affect the Nation’s Midterms
summary: A bitter struggle between Maricopa County’s Republican-dominated Board of Supervisors and a Trump-allied official over control of elections has ramifications far beyond Greater Phoenix.

title: The City That Never Sleeps Suits Up to Play Late-Night Soccer
summary: A pilot program invites people to play soccer under the lights overnight for the duration of the World Cup. The fields have seen no shortage of action.

title: How a Gang of Thieves Pulled Off a Multimillion-Dollar Data Center Heist
summary: The world’s most valuable assets are stored on rows of servers in giant, anonymous buildings. And they can be stolen.

title: Fitness Influencers Are Not Making Men More Fit
summary: Manosphere fitness influencers are the wrong model. Health requires looking at life as a whole, not as an exercise in “optimization.”

title: Trump’s Corruption Stains, From Texas to Maine
summary: Something is wrong if vice is all them and virtue is all you.

title: Cold Plunging
summary: A writer reflects on the healing powers of trying new things.

title: Residents of Spanish Towns Caught in Fierce Blazes Recount Harrowing Escapes
summary: Many say communication was chaotic and even nonexistent as flames tore through the area, taking 12 lives in one of the deadliest wildfires on record in Spain.
